failure_surface: Mismatch between DataFrame columns generated during aggregation and columns written to output range, resulting in incomplete data export
root_cause: The agent performed aggregation on 4 columns (ID, Brand, type, Total) but only wrote 3 columns to the output worksheet (G, H, I), omitting the 'type' column without validation
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Validate that the number of columns in the processed DataFrame matches the intended output column range before writing; if mismatch exists, adjust either the write logic or output range
evidence: Code explicitly writes only 3 cells per row: columns G (ID), H (Brand), and I (Total)
'type' column value is computed and stored in merged_df but never assigned to any worksheet cell
No column count validation between merged_df.columns and target output range was performed

memory_items.title: Output Schema Validation
memory_items.lesson: Always verify that all columns from a processed DataFrame are accounted for in the output write operation
memory_items.generalization: Before writing aggregated or transformed data, compare DataFrame.columns length with target column count and raise error if mismatch detected

memory_items.title: Multi-Column Write Completeness
memory_items.lesson: When iterating over DataFrame rows to write to cells, ensure each column index is mapped to the correct target cell position
memory_items.generalization: Use column enumeration or dictionary mapping to guarantee complete data transfer rather than manually specifying individual column writes

memory_items.title: Post-Aggregation Column Inventory
memory_items.lesson: After groupby, aggregation, or merge operations, audit which columns exist in the result to catch dropped or renamed fields
memory_items.generalization: Log or validate the final DataFrame schema immediately before persisting to external files to prevent silent column loss

edits.content:
## Output Integrity Checks

### Complete Data Transfer Verification
- **Column Count Match:** When writing a DataFrame to an output range, always validate that `len(df.columns)` equals the number of target columns. Log a warning or raise an error if mismatched.
- **Schema Audit After Transformation:** After any aggregation, merge, or filtering operation, inspect `df.columns` to confirm all expected fields are present before writing.
- **Iterative Write Safety:** When using row-wise iteration to write to cells, enumerate columns programmatically rather than hardcoding individual assignments to avoid accidental omissions.
